task: I’m trying to buy a pair of Kobe Bryant sneakers for my son’s birthday, so I want a quick but trustworthy read on what’s coming out soon and what I could actually buy right now. Please start on Sole Collector and look for upcoming Kobe Bryant signature-line releases, pulling the shoe names and release dates from the actual release coverage pages if they’re listed, and leave the most relevant Sole Collector page open so I can glance at it myself. Then check Sneaker News for upcoming Kobe release dates and any useful details like colorways or launch context, and open the main article you used in its own tab so I can compare it side by side with Sole Collector. After that, go to Foot Locker and see whether they have any Kobe entries on their release calendar or product pages, and note whether anything looks upcoming versus currently available; if you find a relevant release or product page, keep that open too so I can visually verify it. Finally, go to Nike and search for Kobe shoes that are actually in stock right now, open any live purchasable product pages you find in separate tabs, and if there’s an available pair, select a common men’s size like 10 if the page allows it just so I can see that it’s really buyable. At the end, give me a concise summary that combines the upcoming release names and dates from Sole Collector, Sneaker News, and Foot Locker with source attribution, plus a note on any in-stock Kobe pairs you found on Nike and which product tabs you left open for me.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent failed to satisfy the rubric requirement. The requirement was to identify upcoming Kobe Bryant signature sneaker releases from Sole Collector. The agent attempted to access `solecollector.com` multiple times but encountered DNS errors (`ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED`) and was unable to load the site. Consequently, it could not identify any release names or dates from Sole Collector. The agent acknowledged this failure in its final summary, stating it could not access the site live.

R2: success - The agent successfully identified upcoming Kobe release dates and details from Sneaker News (specifically the "Nike Kobe 4 Protro Draft Day" article). The agent opened this article in a dedicated tab (tab 4 in the final list) and included the relevant release date (June 26) and details (blind-box style, 14 colorways) in the final summary. The requirement to keep the source page open is satisfied.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Foot Locker release calendar (Step 17) and identified an upcoming release (Nike Kobe V Protro CC PE, June 2, Step 18/29). It also navigated to a product page for the Nike Kobe 9 Elite Low EM (Step 19/32) and confirmed its availability. The agent left these pages open for the user to verify. The status of these items (upcoming vs. available) was correctly determined and reported.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to Nike, identified in-stock Kobe shoes (specifically the Kobe IX Elite Low EM Protro), opened the product page, and performed a size selection for a men's size 10 (as evidenced by the `[checked]` state in the snapshot of the size selection element in step 24). The agent also left the relevant Nike product tabs open for the user.

R5: success - The agent provided a consolidated summary of upcoming Kobe releases (Kobe 4 Protro "Draft Pack" on June 26, Kobe V Protro CC PE on June 2) and in-stock Nike pairs (Kobe IX Elite Low EM Protro). It included source attribution for the information (Complex, Sneaker News, Foot Locker, Nike). It also explicitly noted which tabs were left open for the user. The summary is concise and addresses all components of the rubric.
